landing.subtitle:
Prepare for emergencies. Connect with neighbours. Build community
resilience through mutual aid — even when the
internet is down.

checklist.title: Emergency Checklist
checklist.desc: First aid guides: displacement, checking vitals, CPR, and ABC protocol

map.title: Map
map.desc: View nearby help resources and danger zones on the map

checklist.subtitle: Tap a topic to view the step-by-step first aid guide.

displacement.title: Displacement Skills
displacement.summary: How to safely move an injured patient to a safe area
displacement.text:
If there are dangers around (such as fire, electric dangers or others) the patient has to be moved to a safe place (if it is safe for the first aid provider to do so), where providing the required first aid procedures is possible.

— In case of a possible severe spinal injury: when a patient seems to have a possible serious injury in the spinal cord (in the backbone, either at the neck part or the back part), that patient must not be moved except if that is necessary. When necessary, it must be done as little as possible and very carefully. These precautions avoid many risks of causing further damages for the patient's mobility in the future.

Usually, the patient needs to end up lying down, in a face-up position, on a sufficiently firm surface (for example, on the floor, which allows you to perform the chest compressions of cardiopulmonary resuscitation).

checking.title: Checking Skills
checking.summary: Evaluate the victim's condition and identify main threats to life
checking.text:
Evaluate the condition of the victim, first attending to the main threats to life.

• Check Responsiveness: Tap the patient firmly on the shoulder and shout loudly, such as: "Can you hear me?"

• Control Severe Bleeding: If the victim has a wound that bleeds abundantly, this requires immediate treatment (begin by applying direct pressure to the wound).

• Check Pulse: If the patient does not react, check the carotid pulse by placing two fingers on either side of the neck. Alternatively, check the radial pulse on the wrist, under the thumb.

• Check Breathing: Place your ear near their mouth to listen/feel for air, while watching their chest to see if it rises and falls.

CRITICAL: Do not waste too much time checking. You should take no more than 10 seconds to check for breathing and a pulse before taking further action.

cpr.title: Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation
cpr.summary: Step-by-step CPR procedure for an unresponsive victim
cpr.text:
1. Place the heel of one hand on the center of the chest.

2. Place the other hand on top and interlace fingers.

3. Push hard and fast — at least 2 inches deep, 100–120 compressions per minute.

4. Let the chest rise completely between pushes.

5. If trained, provide 2 rescue breaths after every 30 compressions.

abc.title: ABC Protocol
abc.summary: Airway, Breathing, Circulation — preserve life in emergencies
abc.text:
A — AIRWAY: Gently tilt the head back and lift the chin to open the airway.

B — BREATHING: Look, listen, and feel for normal breathing for no more than 10 seconds.

C — CIRCULATION: Check for severe bleeding. If the person is not breathing normally, begin CPR immediately.


PRESERVING LIFE

The patient must have an open airway — an unobstructed passage that allows air to travel from the mouth/nose into the lungs. Conscious people maintain this automatically, but unconscious patients may be unable to do so.

• The Recovery Position: The patient may be placed laying on their side. This relaxes the patient, clears the tongue from the pharynx, and avoids a common cause of death: choking on regurgitated stomach contents.

• Choking: The airway can become blocked by a foreign object. To dislodge it, use anti-choking methods (such as back slaps, chest thrusts, or abdominal thrusts).

• Transition to CPR: Once the airway has been opened, reassess the patient's breathing. If there is no breathing, or it is abnormal (e.g., agonal breathing), initiate CPR to force air into the lungs and manually pump blood around the body.

list.title: Offline Messages

list.empty: No offline messages have been uploaded yet.

steps.startText: Your main areas are grouped here so you can move quickly during an emergency.
steps.situationText: A neighborhood power outage is affecting nearby buildings. You can ask for water, offer help, and share updates.
steps.forumText: Use the forum when you want to share or follow public neighborhood information.
steps.helpText: Use help requests when someone needs supplies, shelter, transport, or medical help.
steps.infoText: Emergency Info gives you quick steps to check when things feel urgent. Map tools are available after signing in.

featureDesc.forum: Browse community updates, comment on posts, and write a new post.
featureDesc.help: Review nearby needs, create a request, and see it in the list.
featureDesc.profile: Profiles are saved for signed-in community members.
featureDesc.info: Review emergency guidance you can keep handy offline.
featureDesc.messages: Offline messages are available once you sign in on your own account.

dashboard.signInMessages: Sign in to use Offline Messages
dashboard.actionText: Share an update, request help, or check emergency guidance.

steps.feedText: Forum tabs separate broad public updates from hub-specific and urgent conversations.
steps.sortText: Use Newest, Most liked, and Hot to scan recent updates, popular posts, and active conversations.
steps.openText: Click a post to inspect the full update and join the conversation.
steps.createText: Use the new post button to write a community update.
steps.requestText: Forum posts are public updates. If someone needs water, shelter, transport, or medical support, use a help request.

forum.title: Community Forum
forum.subtitle: Community updates from neighbors in your area.
forum.nextBody: In a real emergency, you can use forum posts for public updates. When you personally need supplies, transport, shelter, or medical help, create a help request instead.

steps.typeText: Global posts are broad updates. Hub posts are local. Urgent posts should be reserved for time-sensitive warnings.
steps.titleText: A clear title helps neighbors understand the update before opening the full post.
steps.contentText: Share what happened, where it happened, and what neighbors should do next.
steps.previewText: Check how the post will look before adding it to the forum.
steps.submitText: Save the post when the title and details are clear.

postCreate.title: New Forum Post
postCreate.subtitle: Compose a public update for neighbors.
postCreate.contentPlaceholder: What happened? Where? What should neighbors do?
postCreate.previewBodyFallback: Your post details will appear here.
postCreate.saved: Saved. It will appear in the forum.
postCreate.titleError: Add a short title before saving.
postCreate.contentError: Add details neighbors can act on.
postCreate.situationText: Neighbors need to know where they can charge phones during the outage.
postCreate.readyText: Clear posts reduce confusion and repeated questions.

steps.filterText: Categories help neighbors find requests they can answer quickly.
steps.reviewText: Open a request to see what is needed and how you might respond.
steps.createText: Create a request when someone needs supplies, transport, shelter, or medical help.

helpList.situationText: Neighbors are sharing practical needs after a power outage.
helpList.needsText: Requests stay organized so helpers can quickly find where they are needed.

steps.reviewText: Start with the category, urgency, description, and who posted it.
steps.locationText: The location note helps helpers understand where support is needed.
steps.commentsText: Comments are useful for asking follow-up questions or offering help.
steps.manageText: You can only manage requests you created. When the need is handled, mark it as resolved or remove it if it is no longer needed.

helpDetail.title: Help Request
helpDetail.deleteBody: This removes the request from the list.

steps.scenarioText: Start with the situation so neighbors understand who needs help and why.
steps.titleText: A short title helps neighbors understand the need quickly while scanning a list.
steps.detailsText: Describe who needs help, what is needed, and any safety details helpers should know.
steps.categoryText: These choices help neighbors find urgent needs and understand what they can do.
steps.submitText: Review the details before adding the request to the list.

helpCreate.title: New Help Request
helpCreate.situationText: A neighbor needs drinking water after a power outage.
helpCreate.readyText: Clear details help nearby neighbors understand what kind of help is needed.
helpCreate.info: Add enough detail so nearby neighbors can understand what kind of help is needed.
helpCreate.previewed: Looks good. A request like this gives helpers the main details they need to respond.
helpCreate.descPlaceholder: Explain who needs help and what would be useful.
helpCreate.locationPlaceholder: Example: apartment entrance, street corner, shelter desk
helpCreate.titleError: A clear title helps neighbors understand the need quickly.
helpCreate.descError: Describe who needs help, what is needed, and any safety details.

steps.overviewTitle: Use emergency info as a quick reference
steps.overviewText: This page is for practical guidance you may need during an incident.
steps.checklistText: Tap the checklist card to find step-by-step emergency guidance.
steps.mapTitle: Maps are available after sign in
steps.mapText: The real app also has map tools for nearby gathering points once you are signed in.

emergencyInfo.situationText: You need fast emergency instructions during the same neighborhood outage.
emergencyInfo.actionText: Open the checklist and review the guidance you may need first.
emergencyInfo.mapDesc: Sign in to use live map tools and saved gathering points.
